extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won UAE Tour Stage 2 sprint after QuickStep concealed positioning until final corner at 1200m, emerging from chaos to beat PHILIPSEN.
tactical_patterns: QuickStep kept riders hidden mid-pack while rivals positioned early, only mobilizing after final right-hander at 1200m from finish.
Block headwind until 25km neutralized peloton aggression; crosswind activation after turn triggered positioning chaos, not earlier split attempts.
Israel Premier Tech controlled final corner entry despite no sprinter, suggesting corner-boss-then-sell tactic on compressed highway-to-turn finishes.
Gazprom three-rider breakaway monopolized intermediate sprint points, rendering points jersey competition effectively decided by Stage 2.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: negative: led out but boxed himself after corner, suggesting QuickStep's Morkov-Cavendish axis superior on technical finishes
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: explicitly noted as boxed in at crucial 1200m corner, indicating positioning deficit compared to QuickStep/Alpecin
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: won from non-lead position after chaos, revealing form peak and Morkov's value navigating compressed technical finishes
rider_signals.STRZELECKI Damian: positive: accumulated points jersey lead via breakaway double-dipping intermediate sprints, exploiting peloton's headwind apathy
